title: BC LCFS Average Credit Prices Drop to a Four-Year Low of $195.08, While YTD Volumes Increase 23.8% YoY
content:
Key Takeaways

  • Administrative Monetary Penalties triggered for the first time in 2022 with penalties of CAD 42,7775,261 on 3 number of firms.
    • 2.35 million credits played 3.01 million deficits in 2022, hence 0.66 million credits were used up and depleted the bank.
  • Renewable diesel (41.54%), Ethanol (30.07%), Biodiesel (17.25%), Electricity (8.16%) remain the major credit creators in 2022. Over the assessment years the average CI of major credit generator - Renewable Diesel increased marginally, unlike ethanolâ€™s (second highest credit generator) falling CI. This led to relative decline in credits generated by Renewable Diesel.
  • 2023 sees a credit generation of 11,19,622 and with demand expected to be 33,03,120. By end of year bank will remain marginal.
  • First renewable diesel plant in British Columbia is operational now, generating an output of 3000 barrel/day with a utilization factor of 50%.
author: Adarsh Thakur
